The dissection is taken as far distal as possible towards the secondary carina.

We take care not to drift too far laterally on the airway in order to avoid devascularizing the trachea by cutting its segmental blood supply.

Once the airway has been dissected free, we take transverse diameter measurements at the proximal trachea, the distal trachea, the takeoff of the left main stem bronchus, the takeoff of the right main stem bronchus, and the bronchus intermedius.
These measurements are then used to shape the splint, which is a polypropylene mesh.
We cut this into a Y shape based on the measurements that we took.
we then suture it in to the carina as follows.
Here you can see that the row of four sutures has been placed across the distal trachea, and a row of four sutures has been placed across the right main stem bronchus.

These sutures are placed in a partial thickness fashion, first through the cartilaginous membranous junction, then in a U fashion through the membrane, a second stitch through the membrane, and then finally the contralateral cartilaginous membranous junction.

Here is the completed carinal triangle with a row across the distal trachea, a row across the proximal right main stem bronchus, a row across the proximal left main stem bronchus, and the central membranous suture in the middle of the triangle.
The mesh is then parachuted down into place, and all the sutures are tied.
We then do the right main stem bronchus and bronchus intermedius, and then as a last portion, the left main stem bronchus as distally as possible.
